burnout_drivers.cde_rationale: Directly consumed by Gemma for burnout narrative -- provides the actionable explanation of burnout risk that students need. Without this field, the burnout_score is an opaque number.
burnout_drivers.source_column: Derived from base.onet_context_profiles (9 burnout elements, ranked by normalized value, top 3)

time_pressure.description: The Time Pressure rating for this occupation on a 1-5 scale (CX scale), directly from O*NET Work Context element 4.C.3.d.1. Higher values mean more time pressure. This is one of the 9 burnout elements and is surfaced individually because it is one of the most intuitive burnout indicators for students. Null when no context profile is available.

work_hours.description: The Duration of Typical Work Week rating for this occupation on a 1-3 scale (CT scale), from O*NET element 4.C.3.d.8. A value of 1 means typically less than 40 hours per week, 2 means about 40 hours, 3 means more than 40 hours. Surfaced individually as a key burnout indicator. Null when no context profile is available.

consequence_of_error.description: The Consequence of Error rating for this occupation on a 1-5 scale (CX scale), from O*NET element 4.C.3.a.1. Higher values mean mistakes have more serious consequences (for example, surgeons score high, data entry clerks score low). Surfaced individually as a key burnout indicator. Null when no context profile is available.

activity_importance_mean.description: The arithmetic mean of all 41 Work Activity importance ratings (IM scale, 1-5) for this occupation. Measures overall activity intensity -- how many diverse activities this job involves at high importance levels. Null when no activity profile is available.
activity_importance_mean.source_column: Derived: AVG of base.onet_activity_profiles.importance for all 41 activities

top_5_activities.description: A JSON array listing the 5 most important work activities for this occupation (regardless of whether they are human-intensive or automatable), with their importance ratings. Powers Gemma's occupation descriptions by highlighting what this job actually involves day-to-day. Null when no activity profile is available.

activity_profile_available.description: True if O*NET has Work Activity profile data for this occupation (774 occupations), false for 24 partial-data occupations. When false, hmn_score, top_human_activities, human_activity_count, activity_importance_mean, and top_5_activities are all null.
activity_profile_available.source_column: Derived: EXISTS check on base.onet_activity_profiles for this bls_soc_code

context_profile_available.description: True if O*NET has Work Context profile data for this occupation (774 occupations), false for 24 partial-data occupations. When false, burnout_score, burnout_drivers, time_pressure, work_hours, and consequence_of_error are all null.
context_profile_available.source_column: Derived: EXISTS check on base.onet_context_profiles for this bls_soc_code

confidence_tier.description: A three-level data quality classification for this occupation's work profile: 'high' means full data with less than 5% suppression in both activity and context profiles (773 occupations), 'medium' means full data but 5% or more suppression in either profile (1 occupation -- SOC 29-1241 Ophthalmologists, plus SOC 51-2061), 'low' means partial data (24 occupations). Every row receives a tier.

suppress_pct_activities.description: The percentage of Work Activity profile rows for this occupation where O*NET flagged the data as potentially unreliable (suppress_flag = True). Values range from 0 to 100. Null for 24 partial-data occupations without activity profiles. Used as input to confidence_tier classification.

suppress_pct_context.description: The percentage of Work Context profile rows for this occupation where O*NET flagged the data as potentially unreliable. Values range from 0 to 100. Null for 24 partial-data occupations without context profiles. Used as input to confidence_tier classification.

backs_stats.description: Identifies which FutureProof pentagon stats this table feeds. Always 'HMN' for all 798 rows, indicating that this table provides the Human Edge stat. Documentation metadata field.

backs_bosses.description: Identifies which FutureProof boss fights this table feeds. Always 'AI,Burnout' for all 798 rows, indicating that this table powers both the AI boss fight context and the Burnout boss fight. Documentation metadata field.

source_load_date.description: The date when the source O*NET data was loaded into the raw (Bronze) zone. Pipeline metadata carried forward from Silver. All rows share the same date within a single pipeline run.

promoted_at.description: The exact date and time when this row was promoted to the Gold zone consumable table. Generated at promotion time by the transformer.
promoted_at.source_column: Generated at Gold promotion time via CURRENT_TIMESTAMP

consumable.career_transitions.description: The career transition graph data product for FutureProof. Each row represents a directional similarity relationship between two occupations, indicating they share similar skill and knowledge profiles. This table powers Stage 3 branching in the FutureProof game engine, where students explore related careers they could transition to from a given occupation. 15,944 transitions covering all 798 occupations, enriched with titles and work profile availability flags.

record_id.description: A unique identifier for each career transition, computed automatically from the source and related SOC codes using a deterministic hash function. Format: 'tr-' followed by 16 hexadecimal characters. Stable across pipeline re-runs.
record_id.source_column: Derived from bls_soc_code + related_bls_soc_code via compute_grain_id(prefix='tr')

bls_soc_code.description: The SOC code of the source occupation -- the career that the student is currently considering or working in. Format is XX-XXXX. This is the first component of the composite natural key and the primary query key: 'Given this occupation, what are the similar careers?' Join key to consumable.onet_work_profiles and consumable.occupation_profiles.
bls_soc_code.cde_rationale: First component of composite natural key and primary access pattern for Stage 3 branching queries. Also serves as join key back to work profiles and occupation profiles.

source_title.description: The occupation title of the source (starting) career in the transition pair. Enriched by joining to base.onet_occupations on bls_soc_code. Display label for the Stage 3 branching UI.

related_bls_soc_code.description: The SOC code of the related (destination) occupation -- the career a student could transition to. Format is XX-XXXX. This is the second component of the composite natural key. Join key to consumable.onet_work_profiles and consumable.occupation_profiles for the related occupation's data.
related_bls_soc_code.cde_rationale: Second component of composite natural key. Identifies the destination occupation in the career transition graph. Join key to retrieve HMN/Burnout scores and growth/wage data for the related occupation.

related_title.description: The occupation title of the related (destination) career in the transition pair. Enriched by joining to base.onet_occupations on related_bls_soc_code. Display label for the Stage 3 branching UI.

best_index.description: The similarity rank of this career transition, where 1 means most similar and higher numbers mean less similar (up to 20). Used to order related careers in the Stage 3 branching display -- students see the most similar careers first.

relatedness_tier.description: A three-level classification of how similar the two careers are: 'Primary-Short' (ranks 1-5, most similar -- close career pivots), 'Primary-Long' (ranks 6-10, moderately similar -- stretch career moves), or 'Supplemental' (ranks 11-20, loosely related -- aspirational transitions). Used by the Stage 3 UI for filtering and visual grouping.

is_primary.description: True for career transitions classified as Primary-Short or Primary-Long (the top 10 most similar careers). False for Supplemental transitions. A convenience filter for the Stage 3 branching UI to quickly retrieve the most relevant career transitions.

relationship_type.description: The type of relationship between the two occupations. Always 'similarity' in the current dataset, meaning the relationship is based on occupational similarity (shared skills and knowledge), not observed career moves. This distinction is important: these are careers with similar skill profiles, not necessarily common career paths.

source_has_work_profile.description: True if the source occupation has a full work profile in consumable.onet_work_profiles (meaning HMN and Burnout scores are available). Helps the Stage 3 branching UI know whether it can display HMN/Burnout data alongside the transition.
source_has_work_profile.source_column: Derived: lookup of bls_soc_code in consumable.onet_work_profiles WHERE activity_profile_available = True

related_has_work_profile.description: True if the related (destination) occupation has a full work profile in consumable.onet_work_profiles. Helps the Stage 3 branching UI know whether it can display HMN/Burnout data for the career you might transition to.
related_has_work_profile.source_column: Derived: lookup of related_bls_soc_code in consumable.onet_work_profiles WHERE activity_profile_available = True

backs_feature.description: Identifies which FutureProof feature this table feeds. Always 'Stage3Branching' for all 15,944 rows, indicating that this table powers the Stage 3 career branching tree. Documentation metadata field.

source_load_date.description: The date when the source O*NET data was loaded into the raw (Bronze) zone. Pipeline metadata carried forward from Silver through the career transitions table.

promoted_at.description: The exact date and time when this row was promoted to the Gold zone consumable table. Generated at promotion time by the transformer.
promoted_at.source_column: Generated at Gold promotion time via CURRENT_TIMESTAMP

consumable.program_career_paths.description: The core FutureProof data product mapping school + major + career combinations with pentagon stats and boss fight scores. Each row represents one institution-program-occupation triple, combining College Scorecard program-level outcomes with BLS occupation profiles and O*NET work profiles. Powers Stage 1 and Stage 2 of the FutureProof game engine. 40 columns covering identity, pentagon stats (1-10), boss fight scores (1-10), program context, occupation context, and data quality signals.

record_id.description: Deterministic surrogate key computed via compute_grain_id(row, ['unitid', 'cipcode', 'soc_code'], prefix='pcp'). Format: pcp-<16 hex chars>. Stable across pipeline re-runs.
record_id.source_column: Derived from unitid + cipcode + soc_code via compute_grain_id(prefix='pcp')

unitid.description: IPEDS 6-digit institution identifier. Natural key component (composite with cipcode, soc_code). Source: consumable.career_outcomes.unitid. Every consumer query filters or groups by institution.
unitid.cde_rationale: Natural key component identifying the institution. Required for the core FutureProof query pattern (school + major -> career outcomes). As a Gold consumable key, incorrect or missing values break the MCP layer, frontend rendering, and all downstream comparison queries.

institution_name.description: Official institution name from IPEDS. Display label only; unitid is the authoritative identifier. Source: consumable.career_outcomes.institution_name.

cipcode.description: CIP program code in 4-digit XX.XX format (Scorecard granularity). Natural key component (composite with unitid, soc_code). Source: consumable.career_outcomes.cipcode. The CIP-to-SOC crosswalk join pivots on this field (4-digit prefix match).
cipcode.cde_rationale: Natural key component identifying the academic program. The CIP-to-SOC crosswalk join pivots on this field (4-digit prefix match). Incorrect cipcode values produce wrong career path mappings, corrupting the entire product output.

program_name.description: Human-readable program description. Display label; cipcode is the authoritative identifier. Source: consumable.career_outcomes.program_name.

soc_code.description: SOC occupation code in XX-XXXX format. Natural key component (composite with unitid, cipcode). Source: base.cip_soc_crosswalk.soc_code (via CIP prefix join). The product's entire value proposition depends on correct program-to-career mapping via SOC codes.
soc_code.cde_rationale: Natural key component identifying the occupation. This field joins to BLS occupation_profiles and O*NET onet_work_profiles to populate the five-stat pentagon. Incorrect values map students to wrong careers with wrong stats.

occupation_title.description: Official occupation name. Display label; soc_code is the authoritative identifier. Source: consumable.occupation_profiles.occupation_title (preferred) or consumable.onet_work_profiles.occupation_title (fallback).

soc_major_group_name.description: SOC major group label. Source: consumable.occupation_profiles.soc_major_group_name. Null if BLS LEFT JOIN fails.

stat_ern.description: Earning Power score (1-10). Blended: 60% program earnings rank + 40% occupation wage percentile. Null if either input is null. One of the five pentagon stats defining the product's core visualization.
stat_ern.cde_rationale: Earning Power is one of the five pentagon stats that define the product's core visualization and decision framework. Directly consumed by the MCP layer and frontend pentagon rendering. Blends program-level earnings with occupation-level wage position.
stat_ern.source_column: Derived: 60% program earnings rank + 40% occupation wage percentile

stat_roi.description: Return on Investment score (1-10). Piecewise linear inverse of debt-to-earnings ratio, aligned with Department of Education Gainful Employment methodology. Null if debt_to_earnings_annual is null.
stat_roi.cde_rationale: Return on Investment is one of the five pentagon stats. Maps debt-to-earnings ratio to a 1-10 scale. Directly consumed by the MCP layer and frontend. Also feeds boss_loans_score derivation (inverse relationship).
stat_roi.source_column: Derived: piecewise linear inverse of debt_to_earnings_annual

stat_res.description: AI Resilience score (1-10). Derived from consumable.ai_exposure.stat_res via LEFT JOIN on soc_code. Represents how resilient this occupation is to AI displacement. Higher scores indicate greater resilience. Null if SOC code not found in ai_exposure (occupations without Karpathy coverage).
stat_res.cde_rationale: AI Resilience is one of the five pentagon stats that define the product's core visualization and decision framework. Derived from Karpathy AI exposure scores via consumable.ai_exposure. Directly consumed by the MCP layer and frontend pentagon rendering. Completes the fifth stat in the pentagon.

stat_grw.description: Growth score (1-10). Carried from consumable.occupation_profiles.grw_score_rounded. Represents the labor market demand trajectory for this occupation. Null if BLS LEFT JOIN fails.
stat_grw.cde_rationale: Growth is one of the five pentagon stats. Carried from BLS OOH Gold grw_score_rounded. Represents the labor market demand trajectory. Directly consumed by the MCP layer and frontend pentagon.

stat_hmn.description: Human Edge score (1-10). Carried from consumable.onet_work_profiles.hmn_score_rounded. Measures the degree to which this occupation relies on uniquely human capabilities. Null if O*NET LEFT JOIN fails.
stat_hmn.cde_rationale: Human Edge is one of the five pentagon stats. Carried from O*NET Gold hmn_score_rounded. Measures reliance on uniquely human capabilities. Directly consumed by the MCP layer and frontend pentagon.

boss_ai_score.description: AI Boss strength (1-10). Derived from consumable.ai_exposure.boss_ai_score via LEFT JOIN on soc_code. Represents the AI displacement threat for this occupation. Higher scores indicate greater AI exposure (harder boss fight). Null if SOC code not found in ai_exposure (occupations without Karpathy coverage).
boss_ai_score.cde_rationale: AI Boss is one of five boss fight scores powering the gamified career guidance UI. Derived from Karpathy AI exposure scores via consumable.ai_exposure. Directly consumed by the MCP layer and frontend boss fight rendering. Completes the fifth boss in the gauntlet.

boss_loans_score.description: Student Loans Boss strength (1-10). Computed as 11 - stat_roi. Represents the financial challenge a student faces from debt burden relative to earnings. Null when stat_roi is null.
boss_loans_score.cde_rationale: Student Loans Boss is one of five boss fight scores powering the gamified career guidance UI. Derived as 11 - stat_roi. Directly consumed by the MCP layer and frontend boss fight rendering.

boss_market_score.description: Market Boss strength (1-10). Carried from consumable.occupation_profiles.market_score_rounded. Represents the difficulty of entering this occupation's labor market. Null if BLS LEFT JOIN fails.
boss_market_score.cde_rationale: Market Boss is one of five boss fight scores. Carried from BLS OOH Gold market_score_rounded. Represents the difficulty of entering this occupation's labor market. Directly consumed by the MCP layer and frontend.

boss_burnout_score.description: Burnout Boss strength (1-10). Carried from consumable.onet_work_profiles.burnout_score_rounded. Represents the occupational health risk of this career path. Null if O*NET LEFT JOIN fails.
boss_burnout_score.cde_rationale: Burnout Boss is one of five boss fight scores. Carried from O*NET Gold burnout_score_rounded. Represents the occupational health risk. Directly consumed by the MCP layer and frontend.

boss_ceiling_score.description: Ceiling Boss strength (1-10). Computed as ROUND(10.0 - 9.0 * wage_percentile_education_tier). Represents the earnings growth constraint for this career. Null if wage percentile unavailable.
boss_ceiling_score.cde_rationale: Ceiling Boss is one of five boss fight scores. Derived from wage percentile within education tier. Represents the earnings growth constraint. Directly consumed by the MCP layer and frontend.

earnings_1yr_median.description: Median 1-year post-completion earnings. Subject to privacy suppression. The school-specific earnings signal that feeds stat_ern derivation (60% weight). Also directly displayed to students via the MCP layer as the headline earnings figure.
earnings_1yr_median.cde_rationale: Primary business metric powering FutureProof's core query. Feeds stat_ern derivation (60% weight). Incorrect values corrupt the Earning Power pentagon stat. Also directly displayed to students as the headline earnings figure.

earnings_1yr_p25.description: 25th percentile 1yr earnings across CIP family (effort slider: low focus). Source: consumable.career_outcomes.earnings_1yr_p25.

earnings_1yr_p75.description: 75th percentile 1yr earnings across CIP family (effort slider: high focus). Source: consumable.career_outcomes.earnings_1yr_p75.

debt_median.description: Median cumulative federal loan debt. Subject to privacy suppression. Feeds debt_to_earnings_annual which in turn feeds stat_roi and boss_loans_score.
debt_median.cde_rationale: Primary debt metric. Feeds debt_to_earnings_annual which feeds stat_roi and boss_loans_score. Incorrect values corrupt the ROI pentagon stat, the Student Loans boss fight, and the affordability assessment.

debt_to_earnings_annual.description: Debt-to-earnings ratio: debt_median / earnings_1yr_median. Values < 1.0 manageable, > 2.0 concerning. Aligned with Department of Education Gainful Employment methodology. Null if either input is null.
debt_to_earnings_annual.cde_rationale: Key affordability ratio. Direct input to stat_roi (piecewise linear mapping) and boss_loans_score (inverse). Errors propagate to two pentagon/boss outputs. Also directly consumed by the MCP layer for affordability guidance.

confidence_tier_program.description: Scorecard-level confidence tier. Source: consumable.career_outcomes.confidence_tier (renamed for disambiguation). Values: high, medium, low, or insufficient.

median_annual_wage.description: BLS median annual wage for this occupation. Feeds the occupation wage percentile component of stat_ern (40% weight). Also directly displayed to students as the career-level earnings benchmark. Null if BLS LEFT JOIN fails.
median_annual_wage.cde_rationale: BLS occupation-level median annual wage. Feeds the occupation wage percentile component of stat_ern (40% weight). Also directly displayed to students. Incorrect values corrupt the Earning Power pentagon stat.

growth_category.description: BLS growth classification for this occupation. Values: declining_fast, declining, stable, growing, growing_fast, or booming. Null if BLS LEFT JOIN fails.

employment_current.description: Current employment count for this occupation. Source: consumable.occupation_profiles.employment_current. Null if BLS LEFT JOIN fails.

education_level_name.description: Typical entry-level education requirement for this occupation. Source: consumable.occupation_profiles.education_level_name. Null if BLS LEFT JOIN fails.

top_5_activities.description: JSON string containing the top 5 work activities by importance for this occupation. Source: consumable.onet_work_profiles.top_5_activities. Null if O*NET LEFT JOIN fails.

top_human_activities.description: JSON string containing top activities with highest human edge for this occupation. Source: consumable.onet_work_profiles.top_human_activities. Null if O*NET LEFT JOIN fails.

burnout_drivers.description: JSON string containing top burnout-contributing work conditions for this occupation. Source: consumable.onet_work_profiles.burnout_drivers. Null if O*NET LEFT JOIN fails.

time_pressure.description: Burnout element: time pressure score (1.0-5.0 CX scale). Source: consumable.onet_work_profiles.time_pressure. Null if O*NET LEFT JOIN fails.

work_hours.description: Duration of typical work week (1.0-3.0 CT scale). Source: consumable.onet_work_profiles.work_hours. Null if O*NET LEFT JOIN fails.

match_quality.description: Classification of cross-source join success: full, partial_no_onet, partial_no_bls, or scorecard_only. Derived at Gold time from actual join results. The primary data quality signal for this table.
match_quality.cde_rationale: Classification of cross-source join success. Primary data quality signal -- tells consumers which data sources contributed to each row. Directly consumed by the MCP layer to surface data completeness. Feeds overall_confidence derivation.
match_quality.source_column: Derived at Gold time from actual join results (full/partial_no_onet/partial_no_bls/scorecard_only)

stats_available_count.description: Count of non-null pentagon stats (0-5). Now includes stat_res from ai_exposure, so maximum is 5 when all data sources join successfully.
stats_available_count.source_column: Derived: count of non-null values among stat_ern, stat_roi, stat_res, stat_grw, stat_hmn

bosses_available_count.description: Count of non-null boss fight scores (0-5). Now includes boss_ai_score from ai_exposure, so maximum is 5 when all data sources join successfully.
bosses_available_count.source_column: Derived: count of non-null values among boss_ai_score, boss_loans_score, boss_market_score, boss_burnout_score, boss_ceiling_score

overall_confidence.description: Synthesized quality tier from stats_available_count and match_quality: high, medium, or low. Controls whether the product shows a 'high confidence' or 'limited data' badge.
overall_confidence.cde_rationale: Synthesized quality tier consumed by the MCP layer and frontend to determine how prominently to display results. Incorrect values mislead students about the reliability of career guidance shown.

promoted_at.description: Timestamp when the row was promoted to the Gold consumable table. Generated at promotion time.
promoted_at.source_column: Generated at Gold promotion time via CURRENT_TIMESTAMP

consumable.career_branches.description: The career branching graph data product for FutureProof Stage 3. Each row represents a directional similarity relationship between two occupations, enriched with GRW/HMN/Burnout/Wage stats for both source and target occupations plus computed deltas. Powers the branch tree visualization where students explore related careers they could transition to. 24 columns covering identity, source stats, related stats, stat deltas, and data quality.

record_id.description: Deterministic surrogate key computed via compute_grain_id(row, ['soc_code', 'related_soc_code'], prefix='br'). Format: br-<16 hex chars>. Stable across pipeline re-runs.
record_id.source_column: Derived from soc_code + related_soc_code via compute_grain_id(prefix='br')

soc_code.description: Source occupation SOC code in XX-XXXX format. Natural key component (composite with related_soc_code). Anchors the source node in the career branching graph. Source: consumable.career_transitions.soc_code.
soc_code.cde_rationale: Natural key component identifying the source occupation in the career branching graph. Required for the Stage 3 branch tree query pattern. Incorrect values break the branch tree visualization and misdirect career pivoting guidance.

source_title.description: Source occupation title. Display label; soc_code is the authoritative identifier. Source: consumable.career_transitions.source_title.

related_soc_code.description: Branch target occupation SOC code in XX-XXXX format. Natural key component (composite with soc_code). Identifies the destination node in the branch tree. Source: consumable.career_transitions.related_soc_code.
related_soc_code.cde_rationale: Natural key component identifying the branch target occupation. Required for the career branching query. Joins to occupation_profiles and onet_work_profiles for the enriched stat profile. Incorrect values map students to wrong branch targets.

related_title.description: Branch target occupation title. Display label. Source: consumable.career_transitions.related_title.

best_index.description: Similarity rank (1 = most similar, up to 20). Used to order related careers in the Stage 3 branching display. Source: consumable.career_transitions.best_index.

relatedness_tier.description: Tier classification: Primary-Short (ranks 1-5), Primary-Long (ranks 6-10), or Supplemental (ranks 11-20). Source: consumable.career_transitions.relatedness_tier.

is_primary.description: True for top 10 branches by similarity (Primary-Short and Primary-Long tiers). False for Supplemental. Source: consumable.career_transitions.is_primary.

source_grw.description: Source occupation GRW score (1-10). Source: consumable.occupation_profiles.grw_score_rounded via LEFT JOIN on soc_code. Null if BLS data unavailable.

source_hmn.description: Source occupation HMN score (1-10). Source: consumable.onet_work_profiles.hmn_score_rounded via LEFT JOIN on soc_code. Null if O*NET data unavailable.

source_burnout.description: Source occupation Burnout score (1-10). Source: consumable.onet_work_profiles.burnout_score_rounded via LEFT JOIN on soc_code. Null if O*NET data unavailable.

source_wage.description: Source occupation median annual wage. Source: consumable.occupation_profiles.median_annual_wage via LEFT JOIN on soc_code. Null if BLS data unavailable.

source_res.description: Source occupation AI Resilience score (1-10). Looked up from consumable.ai_exposure keyed by soc_code (source occupation). Null if source SOC code not found in ai_exposure.

source_ai_boss.description: Source occupation AI Boss strength (1-10). Looked up from consumable.ai_exposure keyed by soc_code (source occupation). Null if source SOC code not found in ai_exposure.

related_grw.description: Target occupation GRW score (1-10). Source: consumable.occupation_profiles.grw_score_rounded via LEFT JOIN on related_soc_code. Null if BLS data unavailable.

related_hmn.description: Target occupation HMN score (1-10). Source: consumable.onet_work_profiles.hmn_score_rounded via LEFT JOIN on related_soc_code. Null if O*NET data unavailable.

related_burnout.description: Target occupation Burnout score (1-10). Source: consumable.onet_work_profiles.burnout_score_rounded via LEFT JOIN on related_soc_code. Null if O*NET data unavailable.

related_wage.description: Target occupation median annual wage. Source: consumable.occupation_profiles.median_annual_wage via LEFT JOIN on related_soc_code. Null if BLS data unavailable.

related_growth_category.description: Target occupation growth classification. Values: declining_fast, declining, stable, growing, growing_fast, or booming. Source: consumable.occupation_profiles.growth_category. Null if BLS data unavailable.

related_education_level.description: Target occupation typical education requirement. Source: consumable.occupation_profiles.education_level_name. Null if BLS data unavailable.

related_res.description: Target occupation AI Resilience score (1-10). Looked up from consumable.ai_exposure keyed by related_soc_code (branch target occupation). Null if target SOC code not found in ai_exposure.

related_ai_boss.description: Target occupation AI Boss strength (1-10). Looked up from consumable.ai_exposure keyed by related_soc_code (branch target occupation). Null if target SOC code not found in ai_exposure.

grw_delta.description: related_grw - source_grw. Positive = branch target grows faster. Null if either side null. Range: -9 to 9.

hmn_delta.description: related_hmn - source_hmn. Positive = more human edge at target. Null if either side null. Range: -9 to 9.

burnout_delta.description: related_burnout - source_burnout. Positive = more burnout risk at target. Null if either side null. Range: -9 to 9.

wage_delta.description: related_wage - source_wage. Positive = higher pay at target. Null if either side null.

res_delta.description: related_res - source_res. Positive = branch target is more AI-resilient. Null if either source_res or related_res is null. Range: -9 to 9.

ai_boss_delta.description: related_ai_boss - source_ai_boss. Positive = branch target faces stronger AI boss (more AI-exposed). Null if either side is null. Range: -9 to 9.

branch_has_full_data.description: True if related occupation has both BLS and O*NET data. Derived: related_grw IS NOT NULL AND related_hmn IS NOT NULL.
branch_has_full_data.source_column: Derived: related_grw IS NOT NULL AND related_hmn IS NOT NULL

promoted_at.description: Timestamp when the row was promoted to the Gold consumable table. Generated at promotion time.
promoted_at.source_column: Generated at Gold promotion time via CURRENT_TIMESTAMP

raw.karpathy_ai_exposure.description: AI exposure scores for 342 U.S. occupations, sourced from Andrej Karpathy's US Job Market Visualizer project. Each row represents one occupation, identified by a kebab-case slug, with a 0-10 AI exposure score and a 2-3 sentence rationale explaining the score. Scores were generated by passing full BLS Occupational Outlook Handbook descriptions through Gemini Flash (an LLM) against a structured rubric. This dataset fills the RES (AI Resilience) stat and the Fight AI boss in the FutureProof pentagon. Supplemental fields from the source (median pay, job count, education) are carried for cross-validation against the pipeline's authoritative BLS OOH data.
raw.karpathy_ai_exposure.source: Andrej Karpathy's karpathy/jobs GitHub repository (scores.json + occupations.csv)

slug.description: The unique identifier for each occupation in the Karpathy dataset, written in kebab-case format (lowercase words separated by hyphens). For example, 'financial-analysts' or 'registered-nurses'. This is the grain of the raw table -- every row has exactly one slug, and no two rows share the same slug. The slug links the exposure score (from scores.json) to the occupation metadata (from occupations.csv) during ingest, and serves as the provenance anchor back to the Karpathy source.
slug.cde_rationale: Grain field and source-level unique key. Links scores.json entries to occupations.csv rows during ingest. Required for SOC code resolution in Silver (slug -> SOC mapping). Without correct slugs, the join between scores and occupations breaks at ingest time.
slug.notes: 342 distinct values. All lowercase kebab-case. Grain of the table.

occupation_title.description: The human-readable name of the occupation, sourced from the title column in occupations.csv. For example, 'Financial analysts' or 'Registered nurses'. This title is displayed to end users in the FutureProof frontend (Fight AI boss narrative, career guidance screens) and also serves as the fallback join key for SOC code resolution in the Silver zone when soc_code is null.
occupation_title.cde_rationale: Human-readable occupation identifier for all consumer-facing outputs. Also serves as fallback join key for SOC resolution in Silver when soc_code is null. Dual role as display field and resolution key.

category.description: The broad occupational category that Karpathy assigned to each occupation, based on BLS occupation groups. For example, 'business-and-financial' or 'healthcare'. There are 25 categories in total. Note: these categories are Karpathy's own grouping and do not map 1:1 to standard SOC major groups or any other official BLS taxonomy. Useful for display grouping and filtering but not a join key.
category.notes: 25 distinct categories. Project-specific taxonomy, not standard BLS classification.

soc_code.description: The Standard Occupational Classification (SOC) code identifying which occupation this row represents, in XX-XXXX format (e.g., '13-2051' for Financial Analysts). SOC codes are the federal taxonomy used by the Bureau of Labor Statistics. This field is the primary cross-source join key that connects Karpathy's AI exposure scores to BLS OOH occupations, O*NET task-level data, and (via the CIP-to-SOC crosswalk) College Scorecard programs. Approximately 85% of rows have a SOC code; the remaining rows have null SOC because their occupations.csv entry lacked a code. Those nulls are resolved in the Silver zone via title matching.
soc_code.cde_rationale: Primary cross-source join key for integrating AI exposure scores into the FutureProof pipeline. Bridges Karpathy data to BLS OOH, O*NET, and (via CIP-to-SOC crosswalk) College Scorecard. Without correct SOC codes, exposure scores cannot flow into consumable.program_career_paths or consumable.career_branches.
soc_code.notes: ~52 rows have null SOC codes. Format varies: XX-XXXX (detailed) or XX-XXX0 (broad). Zero malformed SOC codes where present.

exposure_score.description: A score from 0 to 10 indicating how much current AI technology is expected to reshape this occupation. A score of 0 means minimal AI impact; a score of 10 means the occupation's core tasks are highly susceptible to AI-driven change. The score considers both direct automation (AI replacing human tasks) and indirect productivity effects (AI making workers dramatically more efficient). Important context: a high score does NOT predict job disappearance -- it predicts that the nature of the work will change significantly. This score was generated by an LLM (Gemini Flash) evaluating BLS occupation descriptions against a structured rubric, so it is an estimate, not an empirical measurement.
exposure_score.cde_rationale: Primary analytical payload of this data source. Directly derives two Gold-zone metrics: stat_res (AI Resilience) and boss_ai_score (Fight AI difficulty). Completes the fifth stat in the FutureProof pentagon and the fifth boss in the gauntlet.
exposure_score.notes: Effective range 1-10 (score 0 not observed in current data, but rubric permits it). Median: 5. Most common score: 7 (20.5% of rows). LLM-generated, not empirical.

rationale.description: A 2-3 sentence explanation written by an LLM (Gemini Flash) describing the key factors behind the occupation's AI exposure score. For example, a financial analyst's rationale might explain that the role involves heavy computer-based data analysis and model building, which are tasks AI tools are already handling. Every rationale is unique, substantive, and grammatically complete. This field is carried all the way through to the Gold zone and served to end users in the FutureProof frontend as part of the Fight AI boss narrative.
rationale.cde_rationale: Display field served to end users via MCP and the Fight AI boss narrative. The rationale explains WHY an occupation scored as it did, which is more actionable than the numeric score alone. Domain context calls this 'the single most valuable field for LLM-to-user communication.'
rationale.notes: All 342 rationales are unique. Length range: 297-587 characters, mean 412. No nulls, no empty strings.

median_pay_annual.description: The median annual wage for this occupation in U.S. dollars, sourced from Karpathy's occupations.csv (which draws from BLS data). This field is carried forward for cross-validation purposes only -- the pipeline's authoritative source for wage data is the BLS OOH table (raw.bls_ooh / base.bls_ooh). Cross-validation against our BLS OOH data shows perfect alignment ($0 difference) for all matched rows, confirming Karpathy used the same BLS data snapshot.
median_pay_annual.notes: Cross-validation only. Not a primary analytical field. Perfectly matches BLS OOH median_annual_wage for all direct SOC matches.

num_jobs_2024.description: The number of people employed in this occupation as of 2024, sourced from Karpathy's occupations.csv (which draws from BLS data). This field is carried forward for cross-validation and for duplicate SOC resolution in the Silver zone (when multiple Karpathy slugs map to the same SOC code, the row with the highest employment count is kept). Not a primary analytical field.
num_jobs_2024.notes: Used as tiebreaker for duplicate SOC resolution in Silver. Not a primary analytical field.

entry_education.description: The typical level of education required to enter this occupation (e.g., 'Bachelor's degree', 'High school diploma or equivalent'), sourced from Karpathy's occupations.csv. This field is carried forward for cross-validation only -- the pipeline's authoritative source for education requirements is the BLS OOH table (raw.bls_ooh / base.bls_ooh).
entry_education.notes: Cross-validation only. BLS OOH education_typical is the authoritative source.

source_url.description: The URL from which the scores.json file was downloaded. This is pipeline metadata for data provenance -- it records where the source data came from so that the load can be reproduced or audited.
source_url.notes: Pipeline metadata. Value: GitHub raw URL for scores.json.

ingested_at.description: The exact date and time when this row was ingested into the pipeline. This is pipeline metadata used to track when the data was loaded, which is useful for debugging and auditing.
ingested_at.notes: Pipeline metadata. Generated at ingest time.

source_method.description: The method used to acquire this data. Either 'github_download' (fetched directly from GitHub's raw content URL) or 'local_cache' (loaded from a pre-downloaded copy in data/raw/karpathy_cache/ as a fallback when GitHub returns 403 or 429 rate-limiting errors).
source_method.notes: Pipeline metadata. Two valid values: 'github_download' or 'local_cache'.

load_date.description: The calendar date when this data was loaded into the pipeline. This is pipeline metadata used to track which load a row belongs to, especially useful if the source data is refreshed in the future.
load_date.notes: Pipeline metadata. Single value in current load: 2026-04-09.

base.karpathy_ai_exposure.description: Clean, modeled Silver base table of AI exposure scores for U.S. occupations, derived from Andrej Karpathy's LLM-based analysis of how susceptible each occupation is to AI-driven automation. Each row represents one occupation identified by its SOC code. The table starts from 342 Bronze rows and expands broad SOC codes into their detailed counterparts, resolves missing SOC codes via title matching against BLS OOH data, and flags which occupations have confirmed BLS matches. This table feeds the Gold zone AI exposure consumable, which derives the RES (AI Resilience) pentagon stat and the Fight AI boss score used in FutureProof's career guidance products.

record_id.description: A unique identifier for each row, computed automatically from the SOC code (or slug when SOC is null) using a deterministic hash function. This ID is stable — running the pipeline again on the same data produces the same ID for the same row. Format: 'kai-' followed by 16 hexadecimal characters (e.g., 'kai-a1b2c3d4e5f67890'). Useful for tracking specific occupation records across pipeline runs and ensuring idempotent re-processing.
record_id.cde_rationale: Deterministic grain hash that uniquely identifies each row after broad SOC expansion and deduplication. Required for idempotent pipeline re-runs and lineage tracing. Without a correct record_id, pipeline re-runs could produce duplicates or lose rows during Gold zone promotion.
record_id.source_column: Derived from soc_code (or slug when soc_code is null) via compute_grain_id(prefix='kai')

soc_code.description: The Standard Occupational Classification (SOC) code that identifies which occupation this row represents. SOC is the federal taxonomy used by BLS and O*NET to classify all U.S. occupations. The format is XX-XXXX (e.g., 15-1252 for Software Developers). This is the grain of the table and the primary join key for connecting Karpathy AI exposure scores to BLS occupation data, O*NET task-level data, and (via the CIP-to-SOC crosswalk) College Scorecard academic programs. At Silver, SOC codes have been normalized from Bronze: format validated, null SOC codes resolved where possible via title matching against BLS OOH, and broad codes (like XX-XXX0) expanded into their detailed counterparts. Nullable only for the small number of occupations (~5%) where no SOC code could be determined.
soc_code.cde_rationale: Primary cross-source join key for integrating AI exposure scores into the FutureProof pipeline. Bridges Karpathy data to BLS OOH occupations, O*NET task-level data, and College Scorecard programs via the CIP-to-SOC crosswalk. Without correct SOC codes, exposure scores cannot flow into consumable.ai_exposure or any downstream career guidance products.
soc_code.source_column: raw.karpathy_ai_exposure.soc_code (normalized, resolved, and expanded at Silver)

slug.description: The original kebab-case identifier for the occupation from Karpathy's source data (e.g., 'financial-analysts', 'registered-nurses'). This is a provenance field — it traces each row back to its origin in the source scores.json file. At Silver, the slug is no longer a join key (soc_code has taken over that role), but it is retained so that any row can be traced back to the exact source record it came from. Multiple rows may share the same slug after broad SOC code expansion, because one broad-code occupation can be expanded into several detailed-code rows.

occupation_title.description: The human-readable name of the occupation as provided by Karpathy's source data (e.g., 'Financial Analysts', 'Registered Nurses'). This is a display label carried through to Gold zone products and the MCP tool for end-user presentation. At Silver, the soc_code — not the title — is the authoritative identifier and join key. The title is useful for display, search, and debugging but should not be used for matching or joining.

category.description: The broad occupation category grouping assigned by Karpathy, based on BLS occupation families (e.g., 'business-and-financial', 'healthcare-practitioners', 'computer-and-mathematical'). There are 25 categories in total. This is a project-specific taxonomy — it is not a standard BLS classification. Useful for grouping occupations by career family in display interfaces, but not a join key or decision driver for downstream analytics.

exposure_score.description: A score from 0 to 10 indicating how exposed an occupation is to AI-driven automation and change, generated by an LLM analysis of each occupation's tasks. Higher scores mean greater AI exposure (more of the job's tasks could be performed or significantly altered by AI). This is the primary analytical payload of the Karpathy data source. The Gold zone derives two key metrics from this score: the RES (AI Resilience) pentagon stat (formula: MIN(11 - exposure_score, 10)) and the Fight AI boss score (formula: MAX(exposure_score, 1)). The score is carried verbatim from Bronze with no rescaling or transformation at Silver. The effective range in the current data is 1 to 10 (no zeros observed), though the rubric permits 0.
exposure_score.cde_rationale: Primary analytical payload. Directly derives the RES pentagon stat and Fight AI boss score in Gold zone. A corrupted or missing exposure_score renders the entire Karpathy ingest worthless for downstream career guidance products.

rationale.description: A 2-3 sentence explanation generated by an LLM describing why this occupation received its particular exposure score. Each rationale is unique and substantive — it explains the specific tasks, skills, or characteristics of the occupation that make it more or less susceptible to AI automation. This text is served directly to end users via the MCP tool get_ai_exposure() and the Fight AI boss narrative in the FutureProof frontend. It is the single most valuable field for communicating AI exposure insights to students and career advisors, because it provides actionable context that a bare numeric score cannot. Carried verbatim from Bronze with no editing. Minimum observed length is 297 characters.
rationale.cde_rationale: Display field served to end users via MCP tool and the Fight AI boss narrative. The rationale explains WHY an occupation scored as it did, making it more actionable than the numeric score alone. Without it, exposure scores are bare numbers lacking interpretability for career guidance.

bls_match.description: A true/false flag indicating whether this occupation's SOC code was found in the BLS Occupational Outlook Handbook data (base.bls_ooh). True means the occupation exists in FutureProof's BLS ingest and can be joined to employment projections, wages, and other BLS data downstream. False means the SOC code is either null or valid but absent from our BLS data (possible SOC vintage mismatch or a category BLS does not individually track). The Gold zone filters on bls_match = true — only rows with confirmed BLS matches are promoted to consumable.ai_exposure. The DQ rules require at least 90% of non-null SOC rows to have bls_match = true.
bls_match.cde_rationale: Data quality flag that determines whether an occupation's exposure score can flow into Gold zone consumable tables. Gold filters on bls_match = true. A systematic error in bls_match would result in an empty Gold table, breaking the RES stat and Fight AI boss across the entire pipeline.
bls_match.source_column: Derived at Silver by checking soc_code existence in base.bls_ooh

soc_resolved_method.description: A classification label documenting how the SOC code for this row was obtained during Silver zone transformation. There are exactly four valid values: 'direct' means the occupation already had a valid detailed SOC code in the source data (~59% of rows); 'broad_expansion' means the occupation had a broad SOC code (like XX-XXX0) that was expanded into multiple detailed codes by looking up which detailed occupations fall under that broad category in BLS data (~27% of rows); 'title_match' means the occupation had no SOC code but was matched to one by comparing its title against BLS OOH occupation titles (~7% of rows); 'unresolved' means no SOC code could be determined after all resolution attempts (~7% of rows). This field is useful for assessing data quality and confidence — direct SOC matches are higher confidence than broad expansions or title matches.
soc_resolved_method.cde_rationale: Provenance field documenting how each row's SOC code was obtained. Critical for data quality assessment and auditability. The distribution of resolution methods is itself a DQ metric — a shift signals a change in source data quality. Downstream consumers may use this to weight confidence in exposure scores.
soc_resolved_method.source_column: Derived at Silver during SOC resolution (direct, title_match, broad_expansion, or unresolved)

source_load_date.description: The calendar date when the source data was originally loaded into the pipeline's raw (Bronze) zone. This is pipeline metadata — it records when the data was fetched from Karpathy's GitHub repository, not when the LLM analysis was performed. Renamed from 'load_date' in Bronze to 'source_load_date' at Silver for clarity. Useful for tracking which data load a row belongs to, especially if the source is refreshed in the future.

ingested_at.description: The exact date and time when this row was written to the Silver zone base table. This is pipeline metadata generated at transformation time — it records when the Silver processing happened, not when the source data was created or loaded. Useful for pipeline auditing, debugging, and tracking data freshness.

consumable.ai_exposure.description: Gold consumable table providing AI exposure scores, AI Resilience (RES) pentagon stats, and Fight AI boss scores for BLS occupations. Each row represents one occupation identified by SOC code, with Karpathy's LLM-generated exposure assessment and two derived game-mechanic scores. This table completes the fifth pentagon stat (RES) and the fifth boss fight (Fight AI) in the FutureProof system. Only occupations with confirmed BLS OOH matches are included (389 of 419 Silver rows).

record_id.description: A unique identifier for each row, computed automatically from the SOC code. This ID is stable across pipeline re-runs. Format: 'aie-' followed by 16 hexadecimal characters. The 'aie' prefix distinguishes Gold AI exposure records from Silver records (which use 'kai' prefix).
record_id.cde_rationale: Deterministic grain hash that uniquely identifies each row. Required for idempotent pipeline re-runs and lineage tracing. Serves as the stable row identifier for downstream backfill joins.

soc_code.description: The Standard Occupational Classification code in XX-XXXX format identifying the occupation. This is the natural key and grain of the table. Every SOC code in this table has been validated to exist in consumable.occupation_profiles (BLS OOH). Non-null in Gold because only bls_match = true rows from Silver are promoted. This is the primary join key for downstream backfill into program_career_paths and career_branches, and the lookup key for the MCP tool get_ai_exposure().
soc_code.cde_rationale: Natural key and primary join anchor for the AI exposure consumable table. Every downstream consumer queries by soc_code. An incorrect or missing soc_code would sever the linkage between AI exposure data and the rest of the FutureProof pipeline.

occupation_title.description: Karpathy's occupation title for the SOC code, sourced from the BLS Occupational Outlook Handbook via Karpathy's occupations.csv. This is a display label consumed by the MCP tool get_ai_exposure() and the Fight AI boss narrative in the frontend. Not a join key -- soc_code is the authoritative identifier.

exposure_score.description: The original Karpathy AI exposure score on a 0-10 scale, measuring how much current AI technology will reshape this occupation. Higher scores mean more exposure to AI-driven change. A score of 10 means the occupation's core tasks are highly susceptible to AI disruption; a score of 1 means minimal impact. Actual range in Gold is 1-10 (no score-0 rows survived the bls_match filter). This is the source metric from which stat_res and boss_ai_score are derived. Preserved in Gold for transparency so consumers can see the original assessment alongside the derived game-mechanic scores.
exposure_score.cde_rationale: Original source metric from which stat_res and boss_ai_score are derived. Preserved for transparency and auditability. Consumed by MCP tool get_ai_exposure() to provide the raw score alongside derived stats.

stat_res.description: The AI Resilience pentagon stat on a 1-10 integer scale, measuring how resistant this occupation is to AI-driven automation and displacement. Derived from the Karpathy exposure score using the formula MIN(11 - exposure_score, 10). A stat_res of 10 means the occupation is highly resilient to AI (low exposure); a stat_res of 1 means minimal resilience (high exposure). This backs the RES position in the FutureProof five-stat pentagon. Key invariant: stat_res + boss_ai_score = 11 for all rows where exposure_score >= 1.
stat_res.cde_rationale: One of the five core pentagon stats consumed by the FutureProof game engine for pentagon rendering, by Gemma for career resilience narrative, and by the compare screen. An incorrect stat_res would directly misrepresent occupation AI resilience to students.

boss_ai_score.description: The Fight AI boss strength on a 1-10 integer scale, representing the magnitude of the AI automation threat to this occupation. Derived from the Karpathy exposure score using the formula MAX(exposure_score, 1). A boss_ai_score of 10 means the AI boss is extremely strong (high exposure, tough fight for the student); a boss_ai_score of 1 means the AI boss is weak (low exposure, easy fight). Every boss has at least strength 1. Since all Gold rows have exposure_score >= 1, boss_ai_score equals exposure_score for all current rows.
boss_ai_score.cde_rationale: One of the five boss fight scores consumed by the FutureProof game engine for boss fight difficulty calibration and by Gemma for AI threat narrative. An incorrect boss_ai_score would miscalibrate the Fight AI boss.

rationale.description: A 2-3 sentence LLM-generated explanation of the key factors driving this occupation's AI exposure score. Each rationale is unique and describes why the occupation scored as it did -- for example, whether the job involves heavy computer-based work (higher exposure) or hands-on physical tasks (lower exposure). This is the primary display field for the Fight AI boss narrative in the FutureProof frontend and is served via the MCP tool get_ai_exposure(). Minimum observed length is 297 characters; all are substantive and grammatically complete. Carried verbatim from the Karpathy source through Bronze and Silver.
rationale.cde_rationale: Display field served to end users via MCP tool and frontend. The rationale explains WHY an occupation scored as it did, which is more actionable than the numeric score alone. Without it, exposure scores are bare numbers lacking interpretability for career guidance.

category.description: Karpathy's BLS category grouping for the occupation. There are 25 categories such as 'business-and-financial', 'computer-and-mathematical', 'healthcare-practitioners', etc. This is a project-specific taxonomy from Karpathy's BLS data processing, not a standard BLS classification. Useful for display grouping in the frontend and MCP tool responses but not a join key or decision driver.

promoted_at.description: The exact date and time when this row was promoted to the Gold zone consumable table. This is pipeline metadata generated at promotion time -- it records when the Gold transformation happened, not when the source data was created or scored. Replaces Silver's ingested_at in the metadata lineage chain. Useful for pipeline auditing and tracking data freshness.

mcp.get_ai_exposure.description: MCP tool that exposes AI exposure data for a single occupation by SOC code. Performs a point lookup against consumable.ai_exposure (389 rows) and returns 7 fields. No transformation occurs -- direct passthrough from the Gold consumable table. Returns a structured null response when the SOC code is not found.

soc_code.description: Standard Occupational Classification code in XX-XXXX format (e.g., '13-2051'). Required. The tool filters consumable.ai_exposure by exact equality on this field.
SOC occupation code in XX-XXXX format. Echoed from the lookup key. Natural key of the backing table. 389 distinct values, 100% unique.
soc_code.cde_rationale: Natural key and primary lookup anchor. Every consumer queries by soc_code.

occupation_title.description: Human-readable occupation name from Karpathy's source data (e.g., 'Financial Analysts'). Display label for end-user presentation. Not a join key.

exposure_score.description: LLM-generated AI exposure score on a 0-10 scale. Higher means more exposed to AI-driven change. Actual range in current data is 1-10. This is the original Karpathy score from which stat_res and boss_ai_score are derived.
exposure_score.cde_rationale: Source metric for stat_res and boss_ai_score derivations. Consumed by Gemma agent for career guidance narrative.

stat_res.description: AI Resilience pentagon stat on a 1-10 scale. Derived at Gold zone as MIN(11 - exposure_score, 10). High score means resilient to AI. Backs the RES pentagon stat in FutureProof.
stat_res.cde_rationale: One of five core pentagon stats consumed by the game engine and Gemma agent. Incorrect values misrepresent occupation AI resilience to students.

boss_ai_score.description: Fight AI boss strength on a 1-10 scale. Derived at Gold zone as MAX(exposure_score, 1). High score means stronger AI threat. Backs the Fight AI boss in the FutureProof gauntlet.
boss_ai_score.cde_rationale: One of five boss fight scores consumed by the game engine. Incorrect values miscalibrate the Fight AI boss difficulty.

rationale.description: 2-3 sentence LLM-generated explanation of why the occupation received its exposure score. Min observed length 297 characters, max 587, median 404. The most actionable field for career guidance -- explains the reasoning behind the numeric score.
rationale.cde_rationale: Display field explaining WHY an occupation scored as it did. Domain context calls this the single most valuable field for LLM-to-user communication.

category.description: Karpathy's BLS category grouping for the occupation (24 distinct values, e.g., 'business-and-financial', 'healthcare'). Project-specific taxonomy useful for display grouping. Not a join key.

raw.bea_rpp.description: Bureau of Economic Analysis (BEA) Regional Price Parities by state, All Items line code, for year 2024. Each row represents one U.S. state or the District of Columbia, and carries a single index value that measures how expensive that state is relative to the national average (where the national average is 100.0). A state with an index of 110 is 10% more expensive than the national average; a state with an index of 88 is 12% cheaper. This is a tiny (51-row) but load-bearing reference table — it is the authoritative source for every salary adjustment displayed to FutureProof students. Downstream Silver and Gold transformations use this table to compute purchasing_power_multiplier, cost_tier, and pre-adjusted salary display fields, which the MCP tools and frontend consume when presenting career outcomes. IMPORTANT: the current load contains 8 spec-verified 2024 values (CA 110.7, HI 110.0, DC 109.9, NJ 108.8, AR 86.9, MS 87.0, IA 87.8, OK 87.8) and 43 primary-agent estimates in the 86.9–107.9 range, because the BEA API call fell back to the CSV cache. A future refresh with a working BEA_API_KEY will replace the estimates with live values.
raw.bea_rpp.source: U.S. Bureau of Economic Analysis (BEA), Regional Economic Accounts — table SARPP (Regional Price Parities by State), LineCode=1 (All Items), Year=2024. Preferred path: BEA API. Fallback path: data/raw/bea_cache/bea_rpp_2024.csv.

geo_fips.description: The 2-digit zero-padded ANSI/FIPS code identifying the state or jurisdiction for this row. For example, '06' is California, '19' is Iowa, and '11' is the District of Columbia. FIPS codes are the federal standard for identifying U.S. states and are stable over time. This field is the grain of the table (one row per FIPS code) and the sole join key used by every downstream consumer: the frontend maps a student's state selection to a FIPS code and joins back to this table to retrieve the correct RPP. There are intentional gaps at codes 03, 07, 14, 43, and 52 because those FIPS codes were never assigned. All 51 values in the current load are unique and well-formed.
geo_fips.cde_rationale: Primary key and the sole join key for every downstream use of this table. No other column can substitute — geo_name is display-only, and rpp_all_items is not unique (Iowa and Oklahoma tie at 87.8). Also the dedup grain for the Bronze->Silver promote. ANSI/FIPS external standard. Auto-approved as CDE per domain-context.md BEA RPP section.
geo_fips.source_column: BEA SARPP GeoFips (filtered to state-level 2-digit codes)
geo_fips.notes: 51 distinct values, 100% unique. Intentional gaps at 03, 07, 14, 43, 52. DC present at FIPS 11.

geo_name.description: The full human-readable name of the state or jurisdiction, such as 'California', 'Iowa', or 'District of Columbia'. This is the display label that ends up in the MCP tool response and the frontend salary-adjustment UI — for example, 'In California, that's equivalent to about $58,700 in purchasing power.' It also serves as a fallback identifier when a user or the Gemma agent passes a state by name rather than by FIPS code or 2-letter abbreviation. Note that 'District of Columbia' is spelled out in full, not abbreviated as 'DC'.
geo_name.cde_rationale: Display label served by the MCP tool response and the frontend salary-adjustment UI. Fallback identifier when users or LLMs pass a state by name rather than FIPS or abbreviation. Without it, Gemma cannot produce the user-facing narrative. Auto-approved as CDE per domain-context.md BEA RPP section.
geo_name.notes: 51 distinct values, 100% unique. Title-case ASCII. Max length 'District of Columbia' (20 chars); min length 'Iowa'/'Utah'/'Ohio' (4 chars).

rpp_all_items.description: The Regional Price Parity (RPP) index for this state, expressed as a percentage of the national average price level (where the national baseline is 100.0). A value of 110.7 (California) means prices in California are 10.7% higher than the U.S. national average. A value of 86.9 (Arkansas) means prices in Arkansas are 13.1% lower than the national average. This single number covers 'All Items' — the complete basket of consumer goods and services that BEA measures, including housing rents, which are typically the largest driver of state-to-state cost differences. The RPP is an aggregate economic measurement, not a per-person or per-household figure. This is the entire analytical payload of the table — every downstream salary adjustment, cost tier, and frontend display value is derived from this one column. A wrong value here means every salary figure displayed to any student who selects this state will be wrong.
rpp_all_items.cde_rationale: The entire analytical payload of this table. Every derived field in Silver (purchasing_power_multiplier) and Gold (cost_tier, adjusted_30k/50k/75k/100k) is a pure function of this one column. Every salary adjustment displayed to students is national_salary * (100 / rpp_all_items). If this column is wrong for a state, every salary figure displayed to any student who selects that state is wrong. Highest-criticality column in the table. Auto-approved as CDE per domain-context.md BEA RPP section.
rpp_all_items.source_column: BEA SARPP DataValue (parsed from string to double)
rpp_all_items.notes: Observed range [86.90, 110.70]. Mean 96.98, median 96.90, std dev 7.06. 50 distinct values (not 51) because Iowa and Oklahoma legitimately tie at 87.8 — NOT a duplicate. Current load: 8 spec-verified values, 43 primary-agent estimates.

data_year.description: The year the RPP estimate refers to. Every row in the current load is 2024, which is the most recent BEA publication (released February 2026). BEA re-publishes RPPs every February for the prior calendar year, and values shift between years as the underlying price index methodology and consumer basket update. This field is provenance-critical — a row mis-labeled with the wrong year would silently misrepresent the vintage of every downstream purchasing-power figure. When a future annual refresh runs, the DQ rule RAW-BEA-006 must be bumped to the new target year.
data_year.cde_rationale: Provenance-critical temporal dimension. RPP is re-published annually; a mis-labeled vintage silently stales every downstream purchasing-power figure. Governs annual-refresh replacement semantics and distinguishes the 8 spec-verified rows from the 43 estimated placeholder rows in the current load.
data_year.source_column: BEA SARPP TimePeriod (parsed as integer year)
data_year.notes: Constant 2024 across all 51 rows. Bump DQ rule RAW-BEA-006 on annual refresh.

source_url.description: The URL that logically sourced this row — either the BEA API endpoint (with the UserID API key redacted) or the local CSV cache path. This is pipeline provenance metadata that records where the data came from so that the load can be reproduced or audited. Security note: the ingestor must never persist the real BEA UserID query parameter into this column — the URL template or a UserID=REDACTED placeholder must be stored instead.
source_url.notes: Pipeline metadata. Must NOT contain the BEA API key — store redacted URL template.

ingested_at.description: The exact timestamp when this row was ingested into the pipeline. Because the BEA ingest is a single-batch operation (51 rows loaded atomically), this value is identical for every row in a load and effectively acts as a batch identifier rather than a per-row event time. DQ rules check only that it is non-null; they never pin it to a specific value because it changes on every load.
ingested_at.notes: Constant across all 51 rows in a load. Batch stamp, not per-row event time.

source_method.description: Identifies how this row was acquired. Two valid values: 'bea_api' (the live BEA API call succeeded) or 'csv_cache' (the API call failed or the BEA_API_KEY was not available, so the ingestor fell back to reading the pre-downloaded CSV at data/raw/bea_cache/bea_rpp_2024.csv). The current load is 100% csv_cache. A future load with a working API key will be 100% bea_api. Both are valid steady states; neither should be pinned by DQ rules.
source_method.notes: Current load: 100% csv_cache. DQ rule uses IN ('bea_api','csv_cache'), not equality.

load_date.description: The calendar date when this batch load ran. Identical across all 51 rows in a single load. This is operational freshness metadata — the freshness DQ rule (RAW-BEA-016) uses it to check that the data is no more than 400 days old, which allows one full BEA annual publication cycle plus ~5 weeks of slack before raising a stale-data signal.
load_date.notes: Current load: 2026-04-10. Freshness window 400 days (one annual cycle plus slack).

base.bea_rpp.description: Silver-zone state reference table for BEA Regional Price Parities. Exactly 51 rows (50 U.S. states plus the District of Columbia), one row per state, grain = state_fips. Promoted from bronze.bea_rpp with four Bronze passthrough columns (state_fips, state_name, rpp_all_items, data_year), three newly derived Silver columns (state_abbr, census_region, purchasing_power_multiplier), one newly derived provenance column (verification_status), a record_id grain hash, and two pipeline-metadata timestamps (source_load_date, ingested_at). This table is the canonical Silver source of purchasing-power math for every downstream consumer: Gold (consumable.regional_price_parities), the MCP tools (get_regional_price_parity, compare_purchasing_power), and the frontend salary-adjustment display. The table is governed at quality tier 'partial_verification' — 8 of 51 rows are BEA-authoritative for 2024 (CA, HI, DC, NJ, AR, MS, IA, OK) and the other 43 are primary-agent plausible estimates pending a live BEA API refresh. The per-row verification_status column lets every downstream consumer distinguish authoritative rows from estimated rows. This column implements Condition 6 of the Bronze staff-engineer review (governance/approvals/raw-ingest-bea-rpp-staff-review.md). No PII; all columns are state-level aggregates or deterministic transformations of them. No cross-source joins; BEA RPP is orthogonal to the SOC/CIP join graph and joins to careers only at query time on the student's selected state.
base.bea_rpp.source: bronze.bea_rpp (Silver promote — no joins, no normalization, no entity resolution). Upstream of Bronze: U.S. Bureau of Economic Analysis (BEA), Regional Economic Accounts, table SARPP, LineCode=1, Year=2024.

record_id.description: The unique identifier for each row in the Silver table, computed deterministically from state_fips using compute_grain_id(row, ['state_fips'], prefix='rpp'). Each of the 51 rows has a distinct record_id, and the same state always produces the same record_id across runs, which is what makes the Silver promote idempotent. This field exists so the pipeline's dedup and upsert machinery has a stable row key; business users never see it.
record_id.source_column: computed from state_fips via compute_grain_id(row, ['state_fips'], prefix='rpp')
record_id.notes: 51 distinct values. Pure function of state_fips — flagging it as CDE would duplicate the state_fips flag, so it is intentionally not a CDE.

state_fips.description: The 2-digit zero-padded ANSI/FIPS code identifying the state or jurisdiction for this row — for example, '06' is California, '19' is Iowa, and '11' is the District of Columbia. FIPS codes are the federal standard for identifying U.S. states and are stable over time. This field is the grain of the Silver table (one row per FIPS code) and the primary join key every downstream zone (Gold, MCP tools, frontend state selector) resolves back to. The student's state selection on the frontend maps to a FIPS code which joins back to this column through Silver and Gold. Promoted verbatim from bronze.bea_rpp.geo_fips.
state_fips.cde_rationale: Primary key of the Silver table and dedup grain for compute_grain_id. The join anchor that every downstream zone resolves back to. No other column can substitute — state_name is display-only, state_abbr is 1:1-isomorphic-but-derived, rpp_all_items is not unique (Iowa and Oklahoma tie at 87.8). ANSI/FIPS external standard. Carried forward from Bronze CDE set. Auto-approved per domain-context.md BEA RPP section.
state_fips.notes: 51 distinct values, 100% unique. Intentional gaps at 03, 07, 14, 43, 52 (unassigned FIPS codes). DC at FIPS 11.

state_name.description: The full human-readable name of the state or jurisdiction, such as 'California', 'Iowa', or 'District of Columbia'. This is the primary display label served by MCP tool responses (for example, 'state_name': 'California') and by the frontend salary-adjustment UI ('In California, that's equivalent to about $58,700 in purchasing power'). It also serves as a fallback identifier when a user or the Gemma agent passes a state by full name rather than by FIPS code or 2-letter abbreviation — the MCP tool get_regional_price_parity accepts all three. 'District of Columbia' is spelled out in full, not abbreviated to 'DC'. Promoted verbatim from bronze.bea_rpp.geo_name.
state_name.cde_rationale: Primary display label served by MCP tool responses and frontend salary-adjustment UI. Fallback identifier when a user or LLM passes state by name rather than FIPS or abbreviation. Carried forward from Bronze CDE set. Auto-approved per domain-context.md BEA RPP section.
state_name.notes: 51 distinct values, 1:1 with state_fips. Title-case ASCII.

state_abbr.description: The 2-letter uppercase USPS postal abbreviation for the state, such as 'CA' for California, 'IA' for Iowa, or 'DC' for the District of Columbia. This is the identifier the FutureProof frontend is actually built on — the student's state-selector widget stores a 2-letter USPS code, the MCP tool's 'state' parameter accepts it, every URL and query string uses it, and every chart label displays it. Without this column the frontend cannot round-trip a user's state selection back to this row. This field is derived fresh in Silver from state_fips using a static 51-entry FIPS-to-USPS lookup table that is hard-coded in the Silver transformer; the lookup is structural U.S. geography and does not violate the 'no hardcoded entity data' rule.
state_abbr.cde_rationale: The identifier the frontend uses for state display, the MCP tool parameter, and the user's state selector. Primary human-readable key round-tripped from the UI back to this row. Derived 1:1 from state_fips but functionally load-bearing — the product surface area is built on it. Newly flagged CDE in Silver (does not exist in Bronze).
state_abbr.source_column: derived from state_fips via static FIPS→USPS 51-entry lookup
state_abbr.notes: 51 distinct values. All 2 characters, all uppercase, all in the canonical USPS 51-member set.

census_region.description: The U.S. Census Bureau region the state belongs to, exactly one of 'Northeast', 'Midwest', 'South', or 'West'. Census regions are used for regional comparisons in the frontend (for example, 'how does this career play out across the South?') and for the stretch-goal Fight Location Lock boss's regional cost tiering. This field is derived fresh in Silver from state_fips via a static FIPS-to-region lookup using the U.S. Census Bureau's standard assignment. Documented quirk: the District of Columbia (state_fips='11') is placed in 'South' under the Census Bureau classification, despite its Northeast-like cost-of-living profile. This is Census convention, not a bug — any downstream consumer comparing DC to 'Northeast' states must handle this explicitly.
census_region.cde_rationale: Four-valued Census Bureau grouping consumed by frontend regional comparison views and the stretch-goal Fight Location Lock boss. Spec P0-enforces both value domain (IN ('Northeast','Midwest','South','West')) and completeness (all four regions represented). No other column in this table encodes region. Newly flagged CDE in Silver (does not exist in Bronze).
census_region.source_column: derived from state_fips via static FIPS→Census-region lookup
census_region.notes: Exactly 4 distinct values. All four regions represented in the 51 rows. DC-in-South is a documented Census quirk, not a bug.

rpp_all_items.description: The Regional Price Parity (RPP) index for this state, expressed as a percentage of the national average price level where the national baseline is 100.0. A value of 110.7 (California) means prices in California are 10.7% higher than the U.S. national average; a value of 86.9 (Arkansas) means prices in Arkansas are 13.1% lower than the national average. This single number covers 'All Items' — the complete basket of consumer goods and services BEA measures, including housing rents, which are typically the largest driver of state-to-state cost differences. This is the entire analytical payload of the table — every downstream salary adjustment, cost tier, and frontend display value is derived from this one column. Promoted verbatim from bronze.bea_rpp.rpp_all_items with zero transformation; a referential-integrity DQ rule asserts Silver values match Bronze row-for-row.
rpp_all_items.cde_rationale: The entire analytical payload of this table. Every derived field in Silver (purchasing_power_multiplier) and Gold (cost_tier, adjusted_30k/50k/75k/100k) is a pure function of this one column. Wrong values here mis-state every salary figure displayed to any student who selects that state. Highest-criticality column in the table. Carried forward from Bronze CDE set. Auto-approved per domain-context.md BEA RPP section.
rpp_all_items.notes: Observed range [86.90, 110.70]. 50 distinct values (Iowa and Oklahoma legitimately tie at 87.8). Partial verification: 8/51 BEA-authoritative, 43/51 primary-agent estimates.

purchasing_power_multiplier.description: The factor by which a national salary is multiplied to express local purchasing power in this state. Computed in Silver as 100.0 / rpp_all_items and stored as a double. For example, California's multiplier is about 0.9034 — a national $65,000 salary translates to roughly $65,000 × 0.9034 = $58,722 of California purchasing power; Arkansas's multiplier is about 1.1507 — the same salary translates to roughly $74,796 of Arkansas purchasing power. This field exists so Gold, the MCP tools, and the frontend never have to re-derive the math; they all consume a single pre-computed factor. A DQ rule asserts an inverse invariant — purchasing_power_multiplier × rpp_all_items ≈ 100.0 within tolerance 0.01 — which guards computation correctness at rest.
purchasing_power_multiplier.cde_rationale: Pre-computed salary adjustment factor consumed directly by Gemma's MCP tool response, the Gold zone, and the frontend salary displays. Every downstream salary adjustment is a single multiply against this factor. Materialized at rest so the inverse-invariant DQ rule (multiplier × rpp_all_items ≈ 100.0) can guard computation correctness. Newly flagged CDE in Silver (does not exist in Bronze).
purchasing_power_multiplier.notes: DQ range 0.7 ≤ x ≤ 1.3. Observed range approximately [0.9034, 1.1507].

verification_status.description: A per-row provenance label indicating whether this row carries an authoritative BEA value or a primary-agent estimate. Exactly two values: 'bea_official' for the 8 rows whose state_fips appears in the hard-coded allow-list {'06' (CA), '15' (HI), '11' (DC), '34' (NJ), '05' (AR), '28' (MS), '19' (IA), '40' (OK)} — these are the RPP values confirmed against the February 2026 BEA release — and 'estimate' for the other 43 rows, which are primary-agent plausible placeholders pending a live BEA API refresh. Downstream consumers (Gemma, the MCP tools, the frontend) read this column so they can hedge numeric precision on estimated rows, say 'approximately' instead of quoting to one decimal, or refuse to return estimated rows at all in strict mode. This column closes Bronze HIGH-3 at the Silver layer and implements Condition 6 of governance/approvals/raw-ingest-bea-rpp-staff-review.md. When the live BEA API refresh lands, the allow-list expands to all 51 state_fips codes and every row becomes 'bea_official'.
verification_status.cde_rationale: Per-row provenance Gemma uses to hedge numeric precision in MCP tool responses. Direct input to the contract's quality tier — 8 bea_official rows are High tier, 43 estimate rows are Medium tier per domain-context.md. Without this flag downstream consumers cannot distinguish authoritative BEA values from primary-agent placeholder values. Newly flagged CDE in Silver (does not exist in Bronze). Implements Bronze staff-review Ruling 2 / Condition 6.
verification_status.source_column: derived from state_fips via hard-coded 8-state BEA-verified allow-list
verification_status.notes: Current distribution: 8 'bea_official' / 43 'estimate'. P0 DQ rule SIL-BEA-026 asserts COUNT(*) WHERE verification_status='bea_official' = 8.

data_year.description: The year the RPP estimate refers to. Every row in the current load is 2024, the most recent BEA publication (released February 2026). BEA re-publishes RPPs every February for the prior calendar year, and values shift between years as the underlying price index methodology and consumer basket update. This field is provenance-critical — a row mis-labeled with the wrong year would silently misrepresent the vintage of every downstream purchasing-power figure.
