A COUNT(DISTINCT data_year) = 1 DQ rule hardens the supersession-by-replacement contract (Silver is always a single-vintage snapshot). Promoted verbatim from bronze.bea_rpp.data_year.
data_year.cde_rationale: Provenance-critical temporal dimension. RPP is re-published annually; mis-labeled vintage silently stales every downstream purchasing-power figure. Governs annual-refresh replacement semantics. Carried forward from Bronze CDE set.
data_year.notes: Constant 2024 across all 51 rows. Bump the pin on annual refresh.

source_load_date.description: The calendar date when the upstream Bronze load ran, carried through to Silver unchanged. Identical across all 51 rows in a batch. This is operational freshness metadata — the freshness guardrail (inherited from Bronze) checks that source_load_date is no more than 400 days old, which allows one full BEA annual publication cycle plus ~5 weeks of slack before raising a stale-data signal. Promoted from bronze.bea_rpp.load_date (renamed to distinguish it from Silver's own ingested_at).
source_load_date.notes: Renamed from Bronze's load_date. Constant across all 51 rows in a batch. Freshness window 400 days.

ingested_at.description: The exact wall-clock timestamp when this row was written into base.bea_rpp during the Silver promote. Identical across all 51 rows in a single Silver batch — acts as a batch identifier rather than a per-row event time. Useful for operational observability (confirming which Silver run populated a given row) and for lineage reconstruction. IMPORTANT: this is the Silver promote timestamp, distinct from bronze.bea_rpp.ingested_at which is the Bronze batch stamp. Downstream consumers that need to distinguish Bronze ingest time from Silver promote time should reference the fully-qualified table name. Freshness is governed by annual refresh cadence, not by this per-row timestamp.
ingested_at.notes: Distinct from bronze.bea_rpp.ingested_at (per advisory #7). Constant across all 51 rows in a single Silver batch.

consumable.regional_price_parities.description: Gold-zone consumable reference table for BEA Regional Price Parities. Exactly 51 rows (50 U.S. states plus the District of Columbia), one row per state, grain = state_fips. Promoted row-for-row from base.bea_rpp (Silver) with 8 columns carried forward verbatim (state_fips, state_name, state_abbr, census_region, rpp_all_items, purchasing_power_multiplier, verification_status, data_year), 5 newly derived Gold columns (cost_tier, adjusted_30k, adjusted_50k, adjusted_75k, adjusted_100k), a record_id grain hash with the 'rpc' prefix, and a single promoted_at batch timestamp. This is the business-ready, display-ready Gold reference for salary purchasing-power adjustment across FutureProof — every screen that shows a salary number reads from this table, the MCP tools get_regional_price_parity and compare_purchasing_power return rows verbatim from this table, and the Gemma narrative prompts that soften numeric precision on estimated rows key off the verification_status column carried forward from Silver per Bronze staff-review Condition 7. The table is governed at quality tier 'partial_verification' — 8 of 51 rows are BEA-authoritative for 2024 (CA, HI, DC, NJ, AR, MS, IA, OK) and the other 43 are primary-agent plausible estimates pending a live BEA API refresh. Per-row provenance is preserved via verification_status so downstream consumers can distinguish authoritative rows from estimated rows. No PII; all 15 columns are state-level aggregates, deterministic scalar transformations of them, or pipeline metadata. No cross-source joins; BEA RPP is orthogonal to the SOC/CIP join graph.
consumable.regional_price_parities.source: base.bea_rpp (Silver promote — no joins, no cross-source work, no entity resolution, no concept normalization; pure-shaping Gold layer). Upstream of Silver: bronze.bea_rpp and the U.S. Bureau of Economic Analysis (BEA) Regional Economic Accounts API.

record_id.description: The unique identifier for each row in the Gold consumable table, computed deterministically from state_fips using compute_grain_id(row, ['state_fips'], prefix='rpc'). Each of the 51 rows has a distinct record_id, and the same state always produces the same record_id across runs, which is what makes the Gold promote idempotent. The 'rpc' prefix distinguishes Gold consumable record_ids from Silver's 'rpp-' prefix; any mismatched prefix means the compute_grain_id call was passed the wrong prefix argument. Business users never see this field — it exists only so the pipeline's dedup/upsert machinery has a stable row key.
record_id.source_column: computed from state_fips via compute_grain_id(row, ['state_fips'], prefix='rpc')
record_id.notes: 51 distinct values. Pure function of state_fips — flagging it as CDE would duplicate the state_fips flag, so it is intentionally not a CDE. Format: 'rpc-<16 lowercase hex chars>'.

state_fips.cde_rationale: Primary key of the Gold table and dedup grain for compute_grain_id. Join anchor every downstream MCP tool lookup and frontend state selector resolves back to. ANSI/FIPS external standard. Re-affirmed from Silver CDE set; auto-approved per domain-context.md BEA RPP section.
state_fips.description: The 2-digit zero-padded ANSI/FIPS code identifying the state or jurisdiction for this row — for example, '06' is California, '19' is Iowa, and '11' is the District of Columbia. FIPS codes are the federal standard for identifying U.S. states and are stable over time. This field is the grain of the Gold consumable table (one row per FIPS code) and the primary join key every downstream consumer resolves back to — the student's state selection on the frontend maps to a FIPS code which joins back to this column. Promoted verbatim from base.bea_rpp.state_fips.
state_fips.notes: 51 distinct values, 100% unique. Intentional gaps at 03, 07, 14, 43, 52 (unassigned FIPS codes). DC at FIPS 11.

Re-affirmed from Silver CDE set.

rpp_all_items.description: The BEA Regional Price Parity index for this state, All Items line code, expressed on the national baseline = 100.0. cost_tier.cde_rationale: Directly consumer-facing classification. Drives frontend color coding, boss-fight difficulty selection, and Gemma narrative prompts per spec Technical Design. Gold P0 DQ enforces both the 5-value enum domain and row-by-row classification correctness against the frozen CASE expression. No substitute column encodes tier. New Gold-origin CDE flag.
cost_tier.description: A five-bucket classification of the state's cost of living, derived at Gold from rpp_all_items via a frozen CASE expression with left-closed intervals. Values: 'very_high' (rpp >= 108), 'high' (103 <= rpp < 108), 'average' (97 <= rpp < 103), 'low' (91 <= rpp < 97), 'very_low' (rpp < 91). Used by the frontend for color coding salary screens, by the stretch-goal Fight Location Lock boss for difficulty selection, and by Gemma for narrative tone. Tennessee sits exactly on the 91.0 breakpoint and is the canonical witness row for the left-closed convention (TN -> 'low', not 'very_low'). New at the Gold layer — does not exist in Silver or Bronze.
cost_tier.source_column: derived from rpp_all_items via the frozen 5-bucket CASE expression
cost_tier.notes: 5 distinct values. EDA distribution: very_high=4, high=8, average=13, low=11, very_low=15. TN at rpp=91.0 is the left-closed boundary witness (GLD-RPP-024).

adjusted_30k.cde_rationale: Pre-computed display-ready salary adjustment at the $30K national anchor. One of four values the spec identifies as the Gold layer's core purpose: make salary adjustments immediately displayable without client-side math. NOT individual earnings data — this is a state-level reference value at a fixed national anchor. New Gold-origin CDE flag.
adjusted_30k.description: The equivalent local purchasing power, in dollars, of a national salary of $30,000 for this state. Derived at Gold as 30000.0 * purchasing_power_multiplier, rounded to 2 decimals (cents precision — sub-cent precision is false precision for a state-level index). Pre-computed so the frontend and Gemma can display this value without client-side math. IMPORTANT: this is a state-level reference value at a fixed national anchor, NOT an observation of any individual's earnings. New at the Gold layer — does not exist in Silver or Bronze.

mcp.get_regional_price_parity.description: MCP tool that returns the Regional Price Parity (RPP) record for a single U.S. state. Accepts the state as a 2-letter USPS abbreviation, a full state name, or a 2-digit FIPS code (case-insensitive) and performs a point lookup against the Gold table consumable.regional_price_parities (51 rows). Returns 14 response fields covering the state identifiers, the core RPP index, the purchasing power multiplier, the cost-tier classification, four pre-computed adjusted-salary examples ($30K/$50K/$75K/$100K), per-row provenance as data_source (renamed from the Gold column verification_status per Bronze Condition 7), the data vintage year, plus framework envelope fields (row_count, governance.table, governance.quality_tier, governance.owner). Supports a verified_only strict mode that refuses to return rows where data_source='estimate', returning a structured null response instead. Returns a structured null response for unknown states — never raises. No transformations at this zone; the MCP handler is a pure projection + struct bundling of adjusted_Nk columns.

state.description: US state identifier. Accepts any of three forms: the 2-letter USPS abbreviation (e.g., 'CA'), the full state name (e.g., 'California'), or the 2-digit FIPS code (e.g., '06'). Abbreviations and names are case-insensitive. The 51-state universe includes the District of Columbia, which can be passed as 'DC', 'District of Columbia', or '11'. Required.

verified_only.description: If true, the tool refuses to return rows where data_source='estimate' — it will only return data for the 8 BEA-authoritative states (CA, HI, DC, NJ, AR, MS, IA, OK). When a refusal happens, the response has data=null and a message containing 'strict'. Default is false. This is the strict mode that closes Bronze Condition 7's MCP half.

data.state_name.description: Full English name of the U.S. state or jurisdiction, such as 'California', 'Iowa', or 'District of Columbia'. Primary display label in every MCP response and in Gemma's narrative output. When a user passes the state as a full name, the tool resolves it to FIPS, looks up the row, and echoes this exact value back on the wire.
data.state_name.cde_rationale: Carry-forward from Gold CDE. Primary human-readable display label served to Gemma for every MCP response. Fallback identifier when user passes state by full name via tool input normalization.

data.state_abbr.description: The 2-letter USPS postal abbreviation for the state, such as 'CA', 'IA', or 'DC'. This is the identifier the FutureProof frontend widget and URL query strings use. When a user selects a state on the frontend, the selected value is the USPS code, which the MCP tool accepts as input and also echoes back in the response so the caller can round-trip the selection.
data.state_abbr.cde_rationale: Carry-forward from Gold CDE. The identifier the frontend widget and URL query strings use. Round-trip target for user state selection.

data.state_fips.description: The 2-digit zero-padded ANSI/FIPS state code (e.g., '06' for California, '19' for Iowa, '11' for DC). This is the primary key of the backing Gold table and the sole stable join key across all federal statistical products. Returned on the wire so consumer-side code can join back to any other state-indexed product table the downstream frontend or application is using.
data.state_fips.cde_rationale: Carry-forward from Gold CDE. ANSI/FIPS primary key. Anchors the underlying Gold row lookup and is returned on the wire for consumer-side joins back to any other state-indexed product table.

data.census_region.description: The U.S. Census Bureau region the state belongs to. One of four values: 'Northeast', 'Midwest', 'South', or 'West'. Drives frontend regional comparison views and the stretch-goal Fight Location Lock boss that tiers boss difficulty by region. Important quirk: the District of Columbia is classified as 'South' by Census convention despite its Northeast-adjacent geography — consumers comparing DC to Northeast states must handle this explicitly.
data.census_region.cde_rationale: Carry-forward from Gold CDE. Four-valued Census Bureau enum driving frontend regional comparison views and stretch-goal Fight Location Lock boss. Consumer-facing on the wire.

data.rpp_all_items.description: The BEA Regional Price Parity index for the state, expressed as a percentage of the national average price level (national baseline = 100.0). For example, California's 110.7 means prices in California are 10.7% higher than the U.S. national average; Arkansas's 86.9 means prices in Arkansas are 13.1% lower. This is the entire analytical payload of the table — every downstream salary adjustment, cost_tier, and adjusted_examples value is a pure function of this one number. Observed range in the current load: [86.9, 110.7].
data.rpp_all_items.cde_rationale: Carry-forward from Gold CDE. The entire analytical payload of the table. Directly displayed by Gemma. Wrong value here mis-states every salary adjustment for that state. Highest-criticality field in the wire contract.

data.purchasing_power_multiplier.description: The factor by which a national salary is multiplied to reflect local purchasing power in this state. Derived as 100.0 / rpp_all_items. A multiplier of 0.9033 (California) means $50K nationally buys only ~$45,167 worth of goods locally; a multiplier of 1.1390 (Iowa) means $50K nationally buys ~$56,948 worth of goods locally. Returned at FULL precision on the wire (not rounded to 4 decimals) so callers can reproduce the adjusted-salary arithmetic exactly — pinned by MCP-BEA-005. The tool 2 compare_purchasing_power arithmetic reads this field from two rows and derives the adjusted_salary figures.
data.purchasing_power_multiplier.cde_rationale: Carry-forward from Gold CDE. Consumers (including tool 2 itself) use this value to recompute adjusted salary at arbitrary anchors beyond the four pre-materialized examples. Returned at FULL precision per MCP-BEA-005.

data.cost_tier.description: A five-bucket classification of the state's cost of living derived from rpp_all_items. The five enumerated values and their breakpoints are: 'very_high' (RPP >= 108), 'high' (103 <= RPP < 108), 'average' (97 <= RPP < 103), 'low' (91 <= RPP < 97), 'very_low' (RPP < 91). Left-closed intervals. Drives frontend color coding, Fight Location Lock boss-difficulty selection, and Gemma narrative prompts. This is a FutureProof editorial classification, not a BEA publication.
data.cost_tier.cde_rationale: Carry-forward from Gold CDE. Drives frontend color coding, boss-fight difficulty, and Gemma narrative prompts per spec. No substitute wire field encodes tier.

data.adjusted_examples.description: A struct with exactly four keys — '30k', '50k', '75k', and '100k' — holding pre-computed salary adjustments at the $30K, $50K, $75K, and $100K national anchors. Each value is national_salary * purchasing_power_multiplier, rounded to cents. The MCP handler builds this struct client-side from the four Gold adjusted_Nk columns, so the wire contract bundles what the Gold contract stores as four separate columns. The 4-key structure is pinned by MCP-BEA-004. IMPORTANT: these are state-level reference values at fixed national anchors, NOT observed individual earnings data.
data.adjusted_examples.cde_rationale: Carry-forward from Gold CDE — all four Gold adjusted_Nk columns are CDE and bundling into a struct on the wire does not dilute that. Display-ready salary adjustment value set Gemma and the frontend consume directly. P0 DQ at Gold pins each leaf within 1 cent of the formula. NOT individual earnings.

data.data_source.description: Per-row provenance label identifying whether this row's Regional Price Parity value came from an authoritative BEA publication or from a primary-agent plausible estimate. Exactly two values: 'bea_official' (for the 8 BEA-verified states — CA, HI, DC, NJ, AR, MS, IA, OK) or 'estimate' (for the remaining 43 states pending a live BEA API refresh). IMPORTANT RENAME: this field is renamed from the Gold column verification_status to data_source on the MCP wire for Gemma-facing clarity. The rename does not change the enum domain or governance status. Gemma must read this field per row and hedge numeric precision in its narrative when data_source='estimate'. This is the closure of Bronze Condition 7 (MCP half).
data.data_source.cde_rationale: Carry-forward from Gold CDE under the Bronze Condition 7 rename mandate. Per-row provenance Gemma uses to hedge numeric precision in MCP tool responses. Bronze staff-review Condition 7 (MCP half) is closed by this field's presence on every response.
data.data_source.rename_note: verification_status → data_source. Bronze Condition 7 (MCP half). Enum domain and governance status unchanged.

data.data_year.description: The year of the BEA RPP estimate for this row. Constant 2024 across all 51 rows in the current load. Returned on the wire so Gemma can caveat stale vintages in its narrative ('this is based on 2024 data...'). RPPs re-publish annually; a mis-labeled vintage silently stales every downstream salary figure.
data.data_year.cde_rationale: Carry-forward from Gold CDE. Provenance-critical temporal dimension surfaced to Gemma so it can caveat stale vintages.

row_count.description: Response envelope counter added by the MCP framework. Always 1 for a successful single-state lookup; absent in null-case responses (unknown state, strict-mode refusal). Operational observability only — not a decision input for any downstream consumer.

governance.table.description: Framework-added metadata naming the backing Gold source. Constant 'consumable.regional_price_parities' across every response. Not a per-row decision input.

governance.quality_tier.description: Framework-added metadata. Constant 'partial_verification' on every response, inherited verbatim from the Gold table's quality tier. Communicates to Gemma that only 8 of 51 states are BEA-verified and the other 43 are estimates. Gemma uses the per-row data_source field to hedge precision, not this envelope value. Pinned by MCP-BEA-002.

governance.owner.description: Framework-added metadata. Constant '@data-steward' across every response. Operational accountability label identifying the owner of the backing Gold table.

mcp.compare_purchasing_power.description: MCP tool that computes a side-by-side salary comparison between two U.S. states at a user-supplied national salary anchor. Answers the 'What if I move?' scenario. Accepts a salary (positive, less than 10,000,000) and two states (in any of three input forms: USPS abbreviation, full name, or FIPS code) and returns a 19-field response containing: the echoed salary, two nested per-state blocks (each with state_name, state_abbr, adjusted_salary, cost_tier, purchasing_power_multiplier, data_source), the absolute difference in dollars, the percentage difference, plus framework envelope fields (row_count, governance.*). The adjusted_salary, difference, and difference_pct fields are derived at tool-call time from the user input and the Gold-stored purchasing_power_multiplier values. Supports a verified_only strict mode that refuses to return a result when either state is not BEA-authoritative. Input validation handles negative/zero/overflow/non-numeric salary, unknown state, and same-state-twice edge cases — the tool returns structured null responses and never raises. No writes. No Gold transformation — a thin read + arithmetic layer over two Gold rows.

salary.description: The national salary in US dollars being compared between the two states. Must be positive and strictly less than 10,000,000. Invalid values (negative, zero, >= 1e7, non-numeric string, None, NaN, inf, bool) return a structured null response per MCP-BEA-011 — the tool never raises.

state_a.description: The first state to compare. Accepts any of three forms: 2-letter USPS abbreviation, full state name, or 2-digit FIPS code. Case-insensitive. Must resolve to a US state (50 states + DC) after normalization.

state_b.description: The second state to compare. Same input formats as state_a. Must resolve to a DIFFERENT state from state_a after normalization — the same-state check runs on the normalized form, so 'CA' vs 'California' vs '06' are all detected as the same state and rejected per MCP-BEA-012.

verified_only.description: If true, BOTH state_a and state_b must have data_source='bea_official'; otherwise the tool returns a null response with a message containing 'Strict mode' and naming the offending estimate state. Default is false.

data.salary.description: Echo of the user-supplied national salary anchor used for the comparison. Always positive and less than 1e7 (validated before the echo). Returned on the wire so Gemma's narrative stays in sync with the adjusted_salary derivations ('at $65,000, state_a equals...').
data.salary.cde_rationale: Student-facing salary figure being compared. Echoing it back is load-bearing — wrong echo would desynchronize the narrative from the derivation. Flagged as MCP-origin CDE because it is a student-facing salary figure in the wire contract. NOT PII — user-supplied anchor, not observed individual earnings tied to an identified subject.

data.state_a.state_name.description: Full English name of the first comparison state. Display label for state_a in the side-by-side UI and in Gemma's narrative.
data.state_a.state_name.cde_rationale: Carry-forward from Gold CDE. Display label for the first comparison state.

data.state_a.state_abbr.description: The 2-letter USPS abbreviation for the first comparison state. Frontend identifier for state_a.
data.state_a.state_abbr.cde_rationale: Carry-forward from Gold CDE. Frontend identifier for the first comparison state.

data.state_a.adjusted_salary.description: The user-supplied salary adjusted for state_a's cost of living. Derived at tool-call time as round(salary * state_a.purchasing_power_multiplier, 2) using Python's built-in round() (banker's rounding), which matches Gold and DuckDB semantics. THIS IS THE STUDENT-FACING ANSWER FOR STATE_A — the whole purpose of the tool. For the canonical spec example CA vs IA at $65K, this field must equal $58,717.25 for state_a=CA (pinned by MCP-BEA-013). Not individual earnings data — a reference computation at a user-supplied anchor.
data.state_a.adjusted_salary.cde_rationale: NEW MCP-origin CDE flag. THE student-facing adjusted salary figure for state_a — the whole purpose of the tool. Wrong value here misleads the student. Derived live per request from the user-supplied salary and the Gold-stored purchasing_power_multiplier (itself a Gold CDE).

data.state_a.cost_tier.description: Five-bucket cost-of-living classification for state_a. Same enum as the Gold column and tool 1 field: 'very_high', 'high', 'average', 'low', 'very_low'. Drives frontend color coding for the state_a side of the comparison UI.
data.state_a.cost_tier.cde_rationale: Carry-forward from Gold CDE. Drives frontend color coding and narrative tone for state_a in the comparison UI.

data.state_a.purchasing_power_multiplier.description: Full-precision purchasing power multiplier for state_a. Returned so the caller can verify the adjusted_salary derivation and re-apply the multiplier at alternate anchors client-side. Not rounded — full Gold precision.
data.state_a.purchasing_power_multiplier.cde_rationale: Carry-forward from Gold CDE. Returned so consumers can verify the derivation of adjusted_salary and re-apply it at alternate anchors client-side.

data.state_a.data_source.description: Per-row provenance label for state_a. Exactly two values: 'bea_official' or 'estimate'. IMPORTANT RENAME: this field is renamed from the Gold column verification_status to data_source on the MCP wire for Gemma-facing clarity (Bronze Condition 7 MCP half). Gemma reads this field per state to hedge numeric precision in its side-by-side narrative. If verified_only=true and this value is 'estimate', the tool refuses the call with a 'Strict mode' message.
data.state_a.data_source.cde_rationale: Carry-forward from Gold CDE under Bronze Condition 7. Per-state provenance Gemma uses to hedge numeric precision in the side-by-side comparison. Bronze staff-review Condition 7 (MCP half) requires this for each compared state.
data.state_a.data_source.derived_from: consumable.regional_price_parities.verification_status (for the state_a row, RENAMED to data_source on the wire)
data.state_a.data_source.rename_note: verification_status → data_source. Bronze Condition 7 (MCP half). Enum domain and governance status unchanged.

data.state_b.state_name.description: Full English name of the second comparison state. Display label for state_b in the side-by-side UI and in Gemma's narrative.
data.state_b.state_name.cde_rationale: Carry-forward from Gold CDE. Display label for the second comparison state.

data.state_b.state_abbr.description: The 2-letter USPS abbreviation for the second comparison state. Frontend identifier for state_b.
data.state_b.state_abbr.cde_rationale: Carry-forward from Gold CDE. Frontend identifier for the second comparison state.

data.state_b.adjusted_salary.description: The user-supplied salary adjusted for state_b's cost of living. Derived at tool-call time as round(salary * state_b.purchasing_power_multiplier, 2) using Python's built-in round() (banker's rounding). THIS IS THE STUDENT-FACING ANSWER FOR STATE_B. For the canonical spec example CA vs IA at $65K, this field must equal $74,031.89 for state_b=IA (pinned by MCP-BEA-013).
data.state_b.adjusted_salary.cde_rationale: NEW MCP-origin CDE flag. THE student-facing adjusted salary figure for state_b — same rationale as state_a.adjusted_salary but for the second comparison state.

data.state_b.cost_tier.description: Five-bucket cost-of-living classification for state_b. Same enum as state_a.cost_tier. Drives frontend color coding for the state_b side of the comparison UI.
data.state_b.cost_tier.cde_rationale: Carry-forward from Gold CDE. Drives frontend color coding and narrative tone for state_b in the comparison UI.

data.state_b.purchasing_power_multiplier.description: Full-precision purchasing power multiplier for state_b. Returned so the caller can verify the state_b.adjusted_salary derivation and re-apply the multiplier at alternate anchors.
data.state_b.purchasing_power_multiplier.cde_rationale: Carry-forward from Gold CDE. Returned so consumers can verify the derivation of state_b.adjusted_salary and re-apply it at alternate anchors client-side.

data.state_b.data_source.description: Per-row provenance label for state_b. Exactly two values: 'bea_official' or 'estimate'. IMPORTANT RENAME: renamed from the Gold column verification_status to data_source on the MCP wire (Bronze Condition 7 MCP half). Gemma reads this field to hedge narrative precision. If verified_only=true and state_b is 'estimate', the tool refuses with a 'Strict mode' message identifying state_b as the offender.
data.state_b.data_source.cde_rationale: Carry-forward from Gold CDE under Bronze Condition 7. Per-state provenance for state_b. Bronze staff-review Condition 7 (MCP half) requires this for each compared state.
data.state_b.data_source.derived_from: consumable.regional_price_parities.verification_status (for the state_b row, RENAMED to data_source on the wire)
data.state_b.data_source.rename_note: verification_status → data_source. Bronze Condition 7 (MCP half). Enum domain and governance status unchanged.

data.difference.description: The headline dollar-figure answer to 'What if I move?'. Derived at tool-call time as state_b.adjusted_salary - state_a.adjusted_salary. Positive means state_b offers more purchasing power; negative means state_a does. For the canonical spec example CA vs IA at $65K, this field must equal $15,314.64 (pinned by MCP-BEA-013). Wrong value here is the single most visible failure mode of the tool.
data.difference.cde_rationale: NEW MCP-origin CDE flag. Headline dollar-figure answer to the 'What if I move?' user question. Directly displayed in the frontend and narrated by Gemma. Wrong value here is the single most visible failure mode of the tool.

data.difference_pct.description: The percentage form of data.difference, expressed relative to state_a.adjusted_salary. Derived as round(difference / state_a.adjusted_salary * 100, 2). Stable across all salary levels for the same state pair because the ratio ppm_b/ppm_a - 1 is invariant under multiplication by salary (e.g., CA vs IA always yields ~26.08% regardless of whether the anchor is $30K or $100K). Useful for narrative framing ('26% more purchasing power') but intentionally NOT flagged as CDE — it is a pure scalar function of two already-CDE fields, and the decision input is difference, not difference_pct. For CA vs IA at $65K, this field must equal 26.08 (pinned by MCP-BEA-013).
data.difference_pct.cde_rationale: Percentage form of difference — derived purely from difference and state_a.adjusted_salary (both already CDE). Presentation/narrative field, not an independent decision input. Per the CDE principle that derived-from-CDE scalars do not require independent flagging unless they themselves drive a distinct decision, this field is intentionally left un-flagged.

row_count.description: Response envelope counter added by the MCP framework. Always 2 for a successful comparison; absent in null-case responses. Operational observability only.

governance.table.description: Framework-added metadata naming the backing Gold source. Constant 'consumable.regional_price_parities'.

governance.quality_tier.description: Framework-added metadata. Constant 'partial_verification' inherited from the Gold table. Pinned by MCP-BEA-002.
